channel.title: Himalaya's Dev Journal
channel.description: Personal journal, game dev blog, and project portfolio

item.title: Log — 2026-06-17
item.description: Set up a GitHub Actions workflow that runs tests on every PR. Took 10 minutes to configure.

item.title: Building a Game Engine from Scratch in Rust
item.description: Exploring ECS architecture, memory management, and why I chose Rust over C++ for my game engine project.

item.title: PixelQuest — A Retro Platformer
item.description: A canvas-based platformer built from scratch with JavaScript. Features procedural level generation and pixel art assets.

item.title: Log — 2026-05-30
item.description: Read about ‘Event Sourcing’. Might overkill for my projects but fascinating concept.

item.title: Log — 2026-05-29
item.description: Built a small pixel-art editor. Canvas API is surprisingly capable for 2D work.

item.title: My Journey into Procedural Generation
item.description: How Perlin noise, cellular automata, and L-systems helped me create interesting-looking worlds for my game.

item.title: Why I Switched from VS Code to Neovim
item.description: A detailed comparison of my editor experience and why the terminal won out for my workflow.

item.title: Log — 2026-05-10
item.description: Tried out the new Rust 2024 edition features. The async improvements are nice.

item.title: TermDash — Terminal Dashboard
item.description: A CLI dashboard showing system metrics, git stats, and calendar events. Built with Rust and Ratatui.

item.title: Understanding WebAssembly for Game Development
item.description: How WASM lets me run C/C++ game code in the browser and what I learned porting my engine.
